preamble: Diphthongization of a mid-front vowel [e] to [ei] typically occurs as a result of vowel lengthening or as a strategy to maintain contrast in a crowded vowel space.

examples.note: The GVS outcome for long 'a' resulted in [e:] which then diphthongized to [ei] in most modern dialects.
Slight diphthongization of long mid-front vowels in Standard Dutch.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
